user_message: Pulling in context from Fabrikam Residences's meeting transcript -- need to pull the context for the escalation triage.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-01-24
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:05:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:08:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:22:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-O trap (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-O failure mode (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-O -- Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
